title: senml profiles.py: humidity() accepts out-of-range percent_rh values
description:
File: python/src/lichen/senml/profiles.py
Lines: 94-103

The humidity() function accepts any float for percent_rh without validation:

  def humidity(percent_rh: float) -> SenmlRecord:
      return SenmlRecord(n='rel-humidity', u='%RH', v=percent_rh)

Relative humidity is defined as 0-100%. Values outside this range (negative or >100) are physically meaningless.

Similar to existing bug project-LICHEN-aez4 which covers battery percent, but this is a separate function.

Impact:
- Invalid humidity values (e.g., -50%, 200%) silently encoded
- May cause confusion or errors in receiving systems

Fix: Add validation:
  if not (0.0 <= percent_rh <= 100.0):
      raise ValueError(f'percent_rh {percent_rh} out of range [0, 100]')
close_reason: Added range validation to humidity() in python/src/lichen/senml/profiles.py:105 matching voc_index style and bead spec. Tests pass, ruff and mypy clean. Commit c24922833
